chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P No. D-872 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 01. For orders on office objection ‘A’. 02. For hearing of main case. 10.03.2026 Mr. Abdul Rehman Bhutto, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Munawar Ali Abbasi, Assistant A.G. Mr. Nazir Ahmed Bhangwar, D.P.G for the State a/w SIP Ali Hussain Shah of PS New Foujdari, Shikarpur. ************ Through the instant constitutional petition, the petitioner seeks directions for arrest of the accused persons nominated in Crime No.93/2023, registered at Police Station New Foujdari, District Shikarpur. Today, Mr. Abdul Rehman A. Bhutto, learned counsel for the petitioner, Mr. Munawar Ali Abbasi, learned Assistant Advocate General, Sindh, and Mr. Nazir Ahmed Bhangwar, learned Deputy Prosecutor General for the State, along with SIP Ali Hussain Shah of P.S. New Foujdari, Shikarpur, are present. Respondents No.2, 3 and 4 have filed their progress reports, which are taken on record. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that although the F.I.R. was lodged against six accused persons and the final challan has already been submitted, yet some of the nominated accused persons, including two police constables and a school teacher, are still absconding and the police are not making serious efforts for their arrest. Conversely, learned Deputy Prosecutor General, assisted by the Investigating Officer, submits that continuous efforts are being made to arrest the absconding accused persons and that the case is already pending before the competent trial Court. He further submits that the trial Court has already initiated proceedings against the absconding accused persons under Sections 87 and 88, Cr.P.C.

Conversely, learned Deputy Prosecutor General, assisted by the Investigating Officer, submits that continuous efforts are being made to arrest the absconding accused persons and that the case is already pending before the competent trial Court. He further submits that the trial Court has already initiated proceedings against the absconding accused persons under Sections 87 and 88, Cr.P.C. We have heard learned counsel for the parties and perused the record. It appears that investigation in the case has already been completed and the challan has been submitted before the competent trial Court where the matter is presently pending trial. The record further reflects that proceedings under Sections 87 and 88, Cr.P.C. have already been initiated against the absconding accused. In these circumstances, when the matter is already seized by the competent trial Court and statutory proceedings are underway, no case for interference in exercise of constitutional jurisdiction under Article 199 of the Constitution is made out. Consequently, this petition is dismissed. Judge Judge
